Package org.eventb.core.seqprover

Interface IProverSequent

All Known Subinterfaces:
IInternalProverSequent
All Known Implementing Classes:
ProverSequent
public interface IProverSequent
Common protocol for reading Prover Sequents.

A sequent contains:

  • A goal : the predicate to be proven.
  • A set of hypotheses: predicates that can be used to prove the goal.
  • A type environment containing all free identifiers (including carrier sets) appearing in the goal and hypotheses.
The fact that all predicates in the sequent share a common type environment ensures that every free identifier has a unique type and makes the search for fresh free identifiers easier.

In addition to the explicitly stated set of hypotheses, the well-definedness predicates for each hypothesis and the goal may be assumed to prove the goal.

The set of hypotheses contains two subsets:

  • Selected hypotheses : hypotheses thought to be relevant while proving the goal.
  • Hidden hypotheses : hypotheses thought to be strongly irrelevant, redundant, or misleading while proving the goal.
These two subsets are disjoint and need not contain all hypotheses. These subsets have no logical significance. Their sole purpose is to add structure to sequents with a large number of hypotheses. Their sizes are intended to be smaller in comparison with the set of all hypotheses.

Another way of seeing this categorization of hypotheses is that the set of all hypotheses is partitioned into three sets: selected, hidden and default. The default hypotheses are then all the hypotheses that are neither selected nor hidden.

Prover sequents are implemented as immutable. They are created using factory methods in ProverFactory.

To avoid needless copying, modifications to prover sequents are treated internally as incremental constructions. The methods used for incrementally constructing new prover sequents from existing ones are not currently exported, but can be found in IInternalProverSequent.

Since:
1.0

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    containsHypotheses(Collection<Predicate> preds)
    Tells whether the given predicates belongs to the set of hypotheses.
    boolean
    containsHypothesis(Predicate pred)
    Tells whether the given predicate belongs to the set of hypotheses.
    FormulaFactory
    getFormulaFactory()
    Returns the formula factory to use with this sequent.
    Object
    getOrigin()
    Returns the origin of this sequent as an object.
    Predicate
    goal()
    Returns the goal predicate of this sequent.
    Iterable<Predicate>
    hiddenHypIterable()
    Returns an iterator for all hidden hypotheses of this sequent.
    Iterable<Predicate>
    hypIterable()
    Returns an iterator for all hypotheses of the sequent.
    boolean
    isHidden(Predicate pred)
    Tells whether the given predicate belongs to the set of hidden hypotheses.
    boolean
    isSelected(Predicate pred)
    Tells whether the given predicate belongs to the set of selected hypotheses.
    Iterable<Predicate>

    Returns an iterator for all selected hypotheses of this sequent.
    IProverSequent

    Translates this prover sequent using the given formula factory.
    ISealedTypeEnvironment
    typeEnvironment()
    Returns the immutable type environment for this sequent.
    Iterable<Predicate>
    visibleHypIterable()
    Returns an iterator for all visible hypotheses of this sequent.
    Iterable<Predicate>
    visibleMinusSelectedIterable()
    Returns an iterator for all visible but not selected (i.e., deselected) hypotheses of this sequent.

  • Method Details

    • typeEnvironment

      ISealedTypeEnvironment typeEnvironment()
      Returns the immutable type environment for this sequent.

      This type environment contains all free identifiers (including carrier sets) appearing in the sequent and can be used to successfully type check all predicates appearing in the sequent.

      Returns:
      the type environment of the sequent
      Since:
      3.0

    • hypIterable

      Iterable<Predicate> hypIterable()
      Returns an iterator for all hypotheses of the sequent.

      This iterator returns hypotheses in the same order as this set was constructed.

      It is intended to be used in the following way:
      for (Predicate hyp : seq.hypIterable())
      { // do something with hyp
      }

      Returns:
      an iterator for all hypotheses occurring in the sequent

    • containsHypothesis

      boolean containsHypothesis(Predicate pred)
      Tells whether the given predicate belongs to the set of hypotheses.
      Parameters:
      pred - The predicate to search for
      Returns:
      true iff the given predicate is a hypothesis of this sequent.

    • containsHypotheses

      boolean containsHypotheses(Collection<Predicate> preds)
      Tells whether the given predicates belongs to the set of hypotheses.
      Parameters:
      preds - The predicates to search for
      Returns:
      true iff all the given predicates are hypotheses of this sequent.

    • goal

      Predicate goal()
      Returns the goal predicate of this sequent.
      Returns:
      the goal predicate of this sequent.

    • isSelected

      boolean isSelected(Predicate pred)
      Tells whether the given predicate belongs to the set of selected hypotheses.
      Parameters:
      pred - The predicate to search for
      Returns:
      true iff the given predicate is a selected hypothesis of this sequent.

    • selectedHypIterable

      Iterable<Predicate> selectedHypIterable()
      Returns an iterator for all selected hypotheses of this sequent.

      This iterator maintains the order of selected hypotheses. This order is the order in which hypotheses are selected. In case a hypothesis is selected a second time, its order does not get modified.

      It is intended to be used in the following way:
      for (Predicate hyp : seq.selectedHypIterable())
      { // do something with hyp
      }

      Returns:
      an iterator for all selected hypotheses of this sequent

    • isHidden

      boolean isHidden(Predicate pred)
      Tells whether the given predicate belongs to the set of hidden hypotheses.
      Parameters:
      pred - The predicate to search for.
      Returns:
      true iff the given predicate is a hidden hypothesis of this sequent.

    • hiddenHypIterable

      Iterable<Predicate> hiddenHypIterable()
      Returns an iterator for all hidden hypotheses of this sequent.

      This iterator returns hypotheses in the same order as this set was constructed.

      It is intended to be used in the following way:
      for (Predicate hyp : seq.hiddenHypIterable())
      { // do something with hyp
      }

      Returns:
      an iterator for all hidden hypotheses of this sequent

    • visibleHypIterable

      Iterable<Predicate> visibleHypIterable()
      Returns an iterator for all visible hypotheses of this sequent. The visible hypotheses are all the hypotheses that are not hidden. They may be selected or not.

      This iterator returns hypotheses in the same order as this set was constructed.

      It is intended to be used in the following way:
      for (Predicate hyp : seq.visibleHypIterable())
      { // do something with hyp
      }

      Returns:
      an iterator for all visible hypotheses of this sequent

    • visibleMinusSelectedIterable

      Iterable<Predicate> visibleMinusSelectedIterable()
      Returns an iterator for all visible but not selected (i.e., deselected) hypotheses of this sequent.

      This iterator returns hypotheses in the same order as this set was constructed.

      It is intended to be used in the following way:
      for (Predicate hyp : seq.visibleMinusSelectedIterable())
      { // do something with hyp
      }

      Returns:
      an iterator for all deselected hypotheses of this sequent
      Since:
      3.7

    • getFormulaFactory

      FormulaFactory getFormulaFactory()
      Returns the formula factory to use with this sequent.
      Returns:
      the formula factory to use
      Since:
      2.0

    • getOrigin

      Object getOrigin()
      Returns the origin of this sequent as an object. For instance, within the event-B feature, this method will return a handle to the Proof Obligation (IPOSequent) or Proof (IPRProof) from which this sequent is derived.
      Returns:
      an object, or null
      Since:
      2.4

    • translate

      IProverSequent translate(FormulaFactory factory)
      Translates this prover sequent using the given formula factory.
      Parameters:
      factory - the factory to use for translation
      Returns:
      the translated prover sequent
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the translation fails
      Since:
      3.0
